summary: DuckDBAdapter layer uses Effect.sync but the underlying HTTP client setup is async, causing runSync failures in tests
details: The DuckDBAdapter layer (adapter.ts:354) is defined as Layer.effect(Service, Effect.sync(() => makeDuckDBAdapter(...))). The makeDuckDBAdapter function creates a client that makes HTTP calls to DuckDB, which is inherently async. When tests initialize the layer via ManagedRuntime.make().runSync(), it fails with 'An asynchronous Effect was executed with Effect.runSync'. The fix was changing runSync to runPromise in the test, but the root cause is the sync layer definition for an inherently async resource. A future slice should make DuckDBAdapter use Effect.promise or Effect.tryPromise for initialization.
